page.content: The analysis of hydrocarbon compounds is crucial for understanding environmental impacts and potential health hazards associated with various pollutants. The data presented in the table focuses on the quantification of specific n-alkanes and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) in relation to the internal standard Napthalene-d8. This internal standard serves as a critical reference point for ensuring the accuracy and reliability of the measurements obtained for the n-alkanes, including nC-10 Decane and nC-22 Docosane.

page.content: The presence of these compounds is indicative of both natural and anthropogenic sources of pollution, with n-alkanes often originating from fossil fuel emissions and PAHs resulting from incomplete combustion processes. The table delineates the concentrations of various n-alkanes and PAHs, highlighting the significance of naphthalene as a common pollutant that poses environmental and health risks. By comparing the quantified concentrations of these hydrocarbons, stakeholders can better assess the contamination levels in the studied environment and make informed decisions regarding remediation efforts. This data is essential for regulatory compliance and for advancing research aimed at mitigating the adverse effects of these hazardous compounds on ecosystems and human health.
